commit.message:
feat(cc): board-scoped workspace isolation + post-task memory consolidation

Board-scoped workspace:
- CCWorkspaceManager.prepare() accepts board_name + memory_mode params;
  routes workspace root through mc.board_utils.resolve_board_workspace()
  instead of the global agents/{agent}/ path when a board is provided
- _execute_cc_task(): resolves board_id -> board_name before workspace prep (step 0d)
- handle_cc_thread_reply(): same board resolution via tasks:getById; falls
  back to global workspace on any bridge failure

Memory consolidation:
- CCMemoryConsolidator: post-task LLM consolidation into MEMORY.md +
  HISTORY.md + SQLite index; mirrors nanobot end_task_session()
- _execute_cc_task(): fire-and-forget consolidation task after execution
- ChatHandler CC path: same consolidation in finally block (covers errors too)
- Uses tier:standard-low model resolved via TierResolver

Tests:
- TestBoardScopedWorkspace: 4 cases covering board path routing, global fallback,
  memory_mode forwarding, and CLAUDE.md generation
- TestCCBoardScopedWorkspace: 2 executor cases (happy path + bridge failure fallback)
- test_memory_consolidator.py: consolidator unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve crossfade: video swaps 5s earlier, audio transition extended

- Video swap now at 21s before end (was 16s) — next clip visible sooner
- Old clip audio fades out over 9s (was 5s, +4s longer)
- New clip audio fades in 3s after video swap (6s fade-in duration)
- Dimming starts at 26s (was 21s) to match new video swap timing
- TIKKO SKANĒJA overlay moved to 30s before end (before crossfade)

Net effect: you see the next video 5 seconds earlier while the
previous song is still audible, creating a smoother transition.

commit.message:
simplify coverage reporting to no-features vs has-features

The component breakdown table now shows "No Features" / "Has Features"
instead of none/partial/decomposed. Since the default query excludes
closed RFEs, decomposed never appears separately and grouping it with
partial gives more actionable signal.

commit.message:
test(gateway): add coverage CI job and 10 regression tests (CAB-1674) (#1475)

## Summary
- Add `cargo-llvm-cov` coverage job to `reusable-rust-ci.yml` — reports
line coverage % in GHA job summary, runs in parallel with test job
- Add 10 targeted regression tests across 5 gateway modules, each
preventing recurrence of a specific past bug:
  - `auth/oidc.rs` (3): hairpin NAT bypass via `rewrite_base` (PR #819)
- `proxy/llm_proxy.rs` (2): trailing slash in LLM upstream URL causing
double-path 404 (PR #1256)
- `auth/api_key.rs` (1): correct CP API validation endpoint URL (PR
#1249)
- `mcp/tools/mod.rs` (2): `mark_loaded` on failure path preventing retry
storm (PR #1228)
- `quota/budget_cache.rs` (2): shared HTTP client reuse pattern (PR
#1001)

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo check` — compiles
- [x] `cargo test --lib regression` — 10/10 pass
- [x] `cargo test` — full suite pass (1507+ unit + 31 contract + 49
integration + 15 resilience + 22 security)
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- [x] `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` —
clean
- [ ] CI green

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

and last_run. Added columns to CREATE TABLE DDL and ALTER TABLE
fallbacks for existing production tables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add skip exercise feature during workouts

Skip button on each exercise card lets you skip exercises mid-workout.
Skipped exercises collapse, grey out, show amber "Skipped" badge, and
report zero progress. Sets are cleared from the sheet so backend
history/double-progression treats them as if they never happened.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

S1: use scattered random rows instead of contiguous BETWEEN range

Contiguous rows (BETWEEN :start AND :start+24) share 2-3 heap pages and
the same index leaf pages — replay processes them as a few page writes,
producing near-zero overhead even on fast hardware (21K TPS, +0.2ms).

Scattered rows (50 rows spaced 40,000 apart across 2M-row table) each
land on a different heap page and different index leaf pages. With 15
indexes: ~800 distinct page modifications per commit vs ~45 for contiguous.
Replay must do random buffer lookups instead of sequential page access.

commit.message:
Add encrypted connections registry — OAuth/API token wrapping

Tokens stored AES-256-GCM encrypted at ~/.bb/connections.enc. Agent calls
connection.call by name; token injected internally, never in agent context.
Per-connection URL allowlists. Full audit logging with tokens stripped.

CLI: python -m beigebox.connections add|list|remove|test
Key: BB_MASTER_KEY env var or auto-generated ~/.bb/.key (chmod 600)

Co-authored-by: Leo <leo@openclaw.ai>

* feat: add skill-security-auditor POWERFUL-tier skill (#230)

Security audit and vulnerability scanner for AI agent skills before installation.

Scans for:
- Code execution risks (eval, exec, os.system, subprocess shell injection)
- Data exfiltration (outbound HTTP, credential harvesting, env var extraction)
- Prompt injection in SKILL.md (system override, role hijack, safety bypass)
- Dependency supply chain (typosquatting, unpinned versions, runtime installs)
- File system abuse (boundary violations, binaries, symlinks, hidden files)
- Privilege escalation (sudo, SUID, cron manipulation, shell config writes)
- Obfuscation (base64, hex encoding, chr chains, codecs)

Produces clear PASS/WARN/FAIL verdict with per-finding remediation guidance.
Supports local dirs, git repo URLs, JSON output, strict mode, and CI/CD integration.

Includes:
- scripts/skill_security_auditor.py (1049 lines, zero dependencies)
- references/threat-model.md (complete attack vector documentation)
- SKILL.md with usage guide and report format

Tested against: rag-architect (PASS), agent-designer (PASS), senior-secops (FAIL - correctly flagged eval/exec patterns).

commit.message:
Fix persistência SQLite: bind mount fixo no host

Volume nomeado era destruído no redeploy do Coolify.
Substituído por bind mount /opt/gsnetrobo/data:/app/data
para garantir que o banco SQLite persiste entre deploys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(api): hotfixes for #194, #195, #197 (2026-03-05) (#198)

* fix(api): guard charToRaw against list-typed dw$secret (#194)

config::get() can parse unquoted YAML values as lists instead of
character scalars, causing charToRaw() to fail with "embedded nul in
string". Add startup validation to coerce and warn, plus defensive
as.character() at all 4 charToRaw call sites.

Closes #194

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): coerce entity deactivation inputs to correct types (#195)

The deactivation endpoint received is_active as character and
replaced_by as the string "NULL" from JSON payloads, causing type
mismatch errors in dplyr::mutate. Explicitly cast is_active to integer
and convert string "NULL" to NA_integer_ before mutation.

Closes #195

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): invalidate stats cache after entity create/deactivate (#197)

Memoised generate_gene_news_tibble_mem and generate_stat_tibble_mem
served stale data after entity mutations because nothing triggered
cache invalidation. Add memoise::forget() calls after successful
entity creation and deactivation.

Closes #197

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression tests for hotfixes #194, #195, #197

Unit tests covering:
- #194: charToRaw defensive wrapper handles list/character/nested
  secrets, startup validation, JWT round-trip with coerced secret
- #195: is_active string/boolean/integer coercion, replaced_by
  "NULL" string to NA_integer_, full pipeline with tibble mutate
- #197: memoise::forget resets cache, conditional invalidation on
  success only, cache preserved on failure

34 tests total, all pure unit tests (no DB or network required).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(sync): ACK_DELETE/ACK_RENAME Cloud Files callbacks, fix deleted files resurrecting as placeholders

Two fixes:

1. Cloud Files NOTIFY_DELETE and NOTIFY_RENAME callbacks now send ACK_DELETE/ACK_RENAME
   back to Windows via CfExecute. Without this, Windows delete/rename dialogs would
   spin indefinitely waiting for provider response.

2. Scanner's deleted files list (from file_states DB) is now passed through to the
   change detector. Previously, when a file was deleted locally but had no sync cache
   entry (only 48 cached files vs 43000+ total), the detector saw it as a new remote
   file and recreated it as a placeholder. Now deletedLocally overrides ActionDownload
   to ActionDeleteRemote.

commit.message:
feat: implement L0 KrakenSDR, L1 TAK, L2 Meshtastic, L5 ML adapters

- L0 KrakenSDR (435 lines): DoaResult, KrakenResponseParser, MockKrakenInterface,
  KrakenHttpClient, KrakenSdrAdapter (poll loop + MQTT publish), TdoaTriangulator
  (weighted least-squares bearing intersection)
- L1 TAK Client (full): CotParser, TakTcpClient, TakUdpClient, TakClientFactory
- L2 Meshtastic (full): MeshNode, MockMeshtasticInterface, MeshtasticAdapter
- L5 ML Analytics (626 lines): MovementAnalyzer (haversine kinematics),
  AnomalyDetector (z-score population), ThreatScorer (weighted multi-factor 0-1),
  LlmThreatAnalyst (DO GPU 72B FP8 vLLM, HIGH/CRITICAL only),
  MlAnalyticsEngine (10s analysis loop + MQTT publish)
- container.py: wired all 4 adapters in startup/shutdown, added property accessors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add tour completion thank-you flow and feedback form

Tour complete overlay now has two phases:
- Phase 1: "Keep Exploring" (browse more sites) / "I'm Done"
- Phase 2 (after "I'm Done"): thank-you message with "Share Your
  Thoughts" → opens feedback modal, and "Close" to dismiss

FeedbackModal: 5-star rating + suggestion textarea + optional name.
Submits to /api/feedback which emails dina@thorncreativemarketing.com
via SendGrid (email address is server-side only, never exposed).

Persistent feedback button (chat icon) added to tour page header
so walkers can leave feedback any time during the tour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ios): set Manual signing for Release configs + fix deployment target

- App + OneSignalNotificationServiceExtension Release configs: Manual signing
  resolves conflict between Automatic/Development and Apple Distribution
- OneSignalNotificationServiceExtension: fix IPHONEOS_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET
  from 26.0 to 14.0 (26.0 is not a valid iOS version)
- Remove redundant CODE_SIGN_STYLE=Automatic from xcodebuild command

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: upgrade mdstore to 1.0.0 and adopt native comment support (#176)

* feat: upgrade mdstore to 1.0.0 and adopt native comment/flatten features

- Bump mdstore from 0.4.0 to 1.0.0
- Update all generate_frontmatter calls to pass Some(CENTY_HEADER_YAML) as
  the new 4th comment parameter
- Replace format_issue_file post-processing with native mdstore comment support
- Add comment: Some(CENTY_HEADER_YAML) to CreateOptions in item_create handler
- Add comment: None to UpdateOptions literals that listed all fields explicitly
- Remove format_issue_file and format_markdown from utils/format.rs — no
  longer needed now that mdstore handles comment injection natively
- Remove pulldown-cmark and pulldown-cmark-to-cmark dependencies
- Export CENTY_HEADER_YAML from utils/mod.rs instead of format_issue_file
- Add comment: None field to all CreateOptions struct literals in tests

Rustfmt expanded multi-arg generate_frontmatter calls, pushing move_issue()
to 101 lines. Extract cleanup logic into remove_source_issue() helper to
stay under the 100-line limit.

commit.message:
fix(burn): accept any epoch token mint instead of hardcoded ALPHA_SOL_MINT

The burn tool was hardcoded to use ALPHA_SOL_MINT for deriving the token
account and passing to the on-chain program. The program actually accepts
any epoch token from alpha.haus. Now scans all Token-2022 accounts in the
agent PDA wallet and picks the one with the highest balance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
